sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Sevilla have shown mixed form in their last 5 matches, with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, including a strong 4-0 victory against Real Oviedo. Levante UD, on the other hand, are struggling, with no wins in their last 3 matches, managing only 1 win, 1 draw, and 3 losses in their recent 5 outings. This suggests Sevilla hold a clear edge going into the game.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two head-to-head encounters, Sevilla have dominated Levante UD, winning both matches (3-2 and 5-3) with high-scoring affairs. Sevilla have won all 5 of their last matchups against Levante, scoring first in 9 of their last 10 meetings, indicating a psychological advantage for the home side.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Sevilla, positioned 11th with 20 points, have scored 24 goals this season (6th in the league) but struggle defensively, conceding 26 goals (16th). Levante UD, languishing at 20th with just 10 points, have scored only 17 goals and conceded 29, highlighting their offensive and defensive frailties. Sevilla’s superior ball possession (53.9%) and tackling stats (1st in league) further underline their dominance.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Sevilla’s in-form players like Ørjan Nyland (8.3 avg rating) and Adnan Januzaj (7.5 avg rating, 2 goals) could be pivotal, though they miss key contributors like Akor Adams and Ruben Vargas. Levante UD rely on Iván Romero (4 goals) but are without top scorer Etta Eyong (6 goals), which could hamper their attack.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction of a 49% chance for a Sevilla win, combined with their historical dominance over Levante and better current form, the home side are favorites. Levante’s poor league position and ongoing winless streak make an upset unlikely. Expect a competitive match, but Sevilla should capitalize on their home advantage at Ramón Sánchez Pizjuán.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Levante UD leads Sevilla 1-0 in a match played at the Ramón Sánchez Pizjuán. The away side struck just before the break through Iker Losada, capitalizing on one of their key opportunities. Sevilla, despite dominating possession and creating several entries into the final third, failed to convert their chances into goals. Levante, on the other hand, were clinical with their limited opportunities, making the most of their attacking moments.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Sevilla's performance
halftimeSections.text: Sevilla controlled much of the game in terms of ball possession and territorial advantage. They showed intent by frequently entering the final third and pushing into the penalty area, but their finishing let them down. The home side's aerial dominance was evident, and they showed resilience in duels, though discipline issues arose with a couple of bookings. Players like Alexis Sánchez and Djibril Sow had moments in front of goal but couldn't find the net.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Levante UD's performance
halftimeSections.text: Levante UD adopted a more pragmatic approach, focusing on efficiency rather than possession. They created fewer entries into the final third compared to Sevilla but made their chances count, with Iker Losada's goal being the difference. Their defensive organization held firm against Sevilla's pressure, though they struggled in aerial battles. Levante's ability to win duels in midfield helped them maintain a foothold in the game despite being on the back foot for long periods.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking momentum between the two sides. Initially, Levante started with intent, pushing forward in the opening minutes before Sevilla gradually took control with sustained attacking spells around the middle of the half. The home team enjoyed prolonged periods of dominance, particularly in the latter stages of the half, but Levante responded with a critical surge right at the end, culminating in their goal. These shifts indicate that while Sevilla dictated much of the play, Levante were dangerous when they seized their moments.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Sevilla are likely to come out with renewed intensity, desperate to level the scoreline in front of their home crowd. Given their first-half dominance in possession and territory, they will probably push hard for an equalizer, potentially leaving spaces at the back. Levante, buoyed by their lead, may opt for a more defensive setup, looking to protect their advantage while seeking counter-attacking opportunities. The match could hinge on whether Sevilla can convert their chances or if Levante can exploit any defensive lapses.
